decision_target: Whether a dedicated named allocation leaf can provide a calibrated Node 26 allocation-sampling primitive for later product workload comparisons.
source_of_truth: The frozen predeclaration, one raw Node HeapProfiler sampling profile for each of zero, one, and two controls, and deterministic replay output under /tmp/dkr-p4-allocation-leaf.
read_method: Run replay.mjs against raw-zero.json, raw-one.json, and raw-two.json; it rejects metadata drift and sums node.selfSize only for callFrame.functionName equal to allocateOne.

confidence_probability_update.reason: The packet supplied no numeric prior. The posterior is limited to this runtime, object shape, batch, sampler settings, and dedicated-leaf attribution.

controls.retention: Every allocated batch remains reachable through the process-local retained array until profiling stops.

questions_answered: The zero control attributes exactly 0 bytes to allocateOne because it never calls that leaf.
The one control has a positive 12882032-byte attributed signal.
The two control has a 25534256-byte signal and a two-to-one ratio of 1.9821605783932223, within the frozen 1.8 to 2.2 interval.
The signal-to-noise calculation is 12882032, above the frozen minimum of 10.
A raw profile plus a saved replay script can isolate allocations to a dedicated named leaf without using heapUsed deltas.
questions_unanswered: Whether actual pumped-fn lifecycle operations can be isolated behind dedicated named leaves without adding benchmark-only production branches.
The variance of this method across multiple independently authorized observations, runtimes, object shapes, and machines.
Whether retained-memory behavior regresses; this probe measures sampled allocation bytes, not retained heap after cleanup.
Whether any existing or proposed pumped-fn workload has an allocation regression relative to its frozen baseline.
risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The method avoids treating heapUsed delta as allocation bytes.
The result supports only method calibration under the frozen controls; it does not support a pumped-fn performance or allocation-improvement claim.
No observation was repeated after the favorable result, and the observer refuses to overwrite a raw profile.
Independent replay and wall review remain mandatory before the result can steer downstream work.
candidate_pkrs: Add a Node 26 allocation-sampling calibration gate with zero, one, and two controls around dedicated benchmark leaves.
Measure frozen representative pumped-fn workloads with the calibrated method, keeping sampled allocation bytes separate from retained-memory checks.
Add an independent retained-memory cleanup probe for scope and session lifecycle workloads.
downstream_decision: Do not claim product allocation improvement. After independent validation, the orchestrator may fund a PKR that uses this calibrated dedicated-leaf method on frozen product workloads.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Frozen predeclare plus one raw profile per control and deterministic replay
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Inspect predeclare.json and replay the three profiles with replay.mjs

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Worker checkpoint and raw replay output
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Check that the checkpoint limits the conclusion to method calibration and makes no pumped-fn performance claim

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Worker checkpoint scope and raw calibration profiles
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm this probe makes no baseline-versus-candidate product comparison
Require an independent validator record before acceptance

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Frozen predeclare, overwrite-rejecting observer, and raw profile manifest
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Inspect predeclare.json, observe.mjs, and the three raw profile hashes

wall_gate.reason: Worker evidence is not acceptance; independent verification records are absent.

stop_rule_observed: One observation was run for each control. The study stopped after deterministic replay; no favorable repeat was attempted.
worker_boundary: DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.

calibration_boundary.applies_when: Node.js 26.4.0 with V8 14.6.202.34-node.21, a 32768-byte sampling interval, stack depth 64, retained in-heap JavaScript objects, fixed 200000-object batches, and attribution only to the allocateOne frame.
calibration_boundary.does_not_apply_when: Claiming pumped-fn workload allocation, retained-memory behavior, cross-runtime behavior, or a baseline-versus-candidate regression.
